D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Probe Calibration Loop

a colorimeter measures the panel, builds a correction, measures again — each pass drags the response toward the target

A hardware probe (Spyder, i1Display) sits on the screen and reads test patches. Software compares the measured grayscale response and white point against the target — say γ 2.2 and D65 — then writes a correction LUT. The loop repeats, each pass shrinking the residual error.

This panel starts too dark (γ 2.45) and blue-tinted. Step the calibration passes and watch the measured tone curve climb onto the target, or switch to the gray ramp to see the colour cast and gamma error fade toward a clean neutral.
